purpose: Completeness scoring rules for the resume agent. The agent uses these rules to score available candidate data, decide whether to generate a draft or ask follow-up questions, and produce a readiness report before generating any resume.

categories.label: Personal Profile
categories.description: Contact and identity information required for any resume header.
categories.scoring_note: Category score = sum of points for present fields. Maximum is 100 (all fields present). Missing a critical field caps the resume at draft_ready regardless of total score.

follow_up_questions.full_name: What is your full name as it should appear on your resume?
follow_up_questions.email: What is your professional email address?
follow_up_questions.github_url: What is your GitHub profile URL?
follow_up_questions.phone: What phone number should appear on your resume?
follow_up_questions.location: What city or region should appear on your resume?
follow_up_questions.linkedin_url: What is your LinkedIn profile URL?
follow_up_questions.portfolio_url: Do you have a personal site or portfolio URL to include?

categories.label: Project Proof
categories.description: Structured evidence for each project listed on the resume. Scored per project; the category score is the average of the top 3 project scores (or all projects if fewer than 3 exist).
categories.scoring_note: Score each project independently (sum of points for present fields, max 100). Category score = average of top 3 project scores. Zero projects = category score of 0. Missing resume_bullets on any project is the most common reason for a low score here.

follow_up_questions.project_name: What projects should appear on your resume?
follow_up_questions.tech_stack: What technologies did you use in {project_name}?
follow_up_questions.resume_bullets: What are the 2-3 strongest things you built or achieved in {project_name}?
follow_up_questions.github_url: Is there a GitHub repository link for {project_name}?
follow_up_questions.what_you_built: What did you personally own and build in {project_name}?
follow_up_questions.architecture_note: Can you describe the architecture or a key design decision in {project_name} in one sentence?

categories.label: Role Targeting
categories.description: How clearly the resume is aimed at a specific role family and company type. Affects which projects, skills, and keywords the agent emphasises.

fields.label: Target company type (MNC, startup, AI company)

categories.scoring_note: Category score = sum of points for present fields. target_profile is the only critical field. Without it the agent cannot choose which developing_profile to align to.

follow_up_questions.target_profile: Which role are you targeting? (e.g. backend engineer, AI product engineer, full-stack engineer)
follow_up_questions.company_type: Are you primarily targeting MNCs, startups, or AI companies for this resume?
follow_up_questions.ats_keywords_list: Are there specific job description keywords you want to optimise for?
follow_up_questions.section_priority: Should projects come before or after the skills section in this resume?

categories.label: Skills Evidence
categories.description: Whether claimed skills are backed by project or work evidence. Prevents listing skills with nothing to point to.

fields.label: Skills with at least one project reference
Skills marked with honest proficiency where needed

categories.scoring_note: skills_list score is binary (present = 25 points). skills_with_project_backing score = (count of skills backed by a project / total skills claimed) * 50. A skills list with no project references scores 0 for that field. skills_with_honest_level is present if the candidate has flagged at least one skill as beginner or intermediate.

follow_up_questions.skills_list: What are the main technical skills you want to list on your resume?
follow_up_questions.skills_with_project_backing: Which of your listed skills can you back up with a specific project or work example?
follow_up_questions.skills_with_honest_level: Are there any skills where you would describe yourself as a beginner or intermediate rather than proficient?

categories.label: Links and Demos
categories.description: Live URLs, screenshots, and demo evidence that make projects verifiable. No field here is critical — absence is a warning, not a hard blocker — but low scores meaningfully reduce the overall application_ready score.

fields.label: At least one project GitHub URL
At least one live project URL

categories.scoring_note: Category score = sum of points for present or confirmed fields. The at_least_one_live_demo field alone is worth 40 points; missing it is the single biggest drag on this category.

follow_up_questions.at_least_one_github_url: Which of your projects have public GitHub repositories?
follow_up_questions.at_least_one_live_demo: Do any of your projects have a live URL or deployed demo link?
follow_up_questions.screenshots_available: Do you have screenshots or a video walkthrough for any project?
follow_up_questions.readme_quality: Do your GitHub READMEs include a project description, setup steps, and screenshots?

categories.label: ATS Readiness
categories.description: Whether the resume content is likely to pass applicant tracking system filters. Checked last — content quality matters more than ATS optimisation.

fields.label: Role-relevant keywords in technical skills section
Education section complete with degree and dates
All dates in a consistent standard format

categories.scoring_note: Category score = sum of points for confirmed fields. None are critical — a low ATS score is a recommendation, not a blocker.

follow_up_questions.keywords_in_skills: Which keywords from target job descriptions should be matched in your skills section?
follow_up_questions.education_complete: What is your degree, institution, and expected graduation date?
follow_up_questions.consistent_dates: Do all your project and work dates follow a consistent format (e.g. Month Year)?
follow_up_questions.single_column_layout: Will this resume use a single-column layout, or a multi-column design?

not_ready.description: Insufficient data to generate a useful resume. Ask follow-up questions before generating anything.
requirements: OR any critical field in personal_profile is missing

draft_ready.description: Enough data to produce a rough draft with visible gaps. Generate the draft and include a gap report highlighting what is missing.
requirements: at least 1 project with project_name, tech_stack, and at least 1 resume bullet

application_ready.description: Enough data to produce a polished resume ready to submit to employers.
requirements: at least 2 projects with full project_name, tech_stack, and resume_bullets
no critical fields missing across any category
at least one github_url present in project_proof

scoring_output_format.description: The agent must produce this structure before generating any resume. Use it to communicate the current state to the user and decide which action to take.

recommended_follow_up_questions: What are the 2-3 strongest things you built or achieved in Agent-s?
What technologies did you use in fin_Tech?
